# Do What You Can Live With

> Brandi Mac  MSN, APRN, ACNP-BC is a critical care nurse practitioner, author, and harm reduction advocate helping families navigate addiction with evidence-based guidance and lived experience. Her book, "Do What You Can Live With," is recommended reading at Caron Treatment Centers, one of the nation's most respected addiction treatment programs. She has reached over 400,000 families across platforms.

## About
- [About Brandi Mac](https://brandimac.com/about): Clinical credentials (MSN, APRN, ACNP-BC), critical care nurse practitioner at HCA Healthcare, lived experience as a mother navigating a child's fentanyl use, professional speaker, and harm reduction advocate.
- [Contact](https://brandimac.com/contact): Brandi@brandimac.com

## The Book
- [Do What You Can Live With](https://brandimac.com/book): A survival guide for families navigating addiction, grief, and impossible choices. 322 pages. Recommended reading at Caron Treatment Centers. Covers boundary-setting, harm reduction for families, navigating the treatment industry, and finding peace without giving up hope.

## Free Tools and Resources
- [Treatment Navigator](https://brandimac.com/treatment-navigator): Free interactive tool that walks families through finding and vetting addiction treatment centers. Includes red flag identification, questions to ask every center, insurance navigation, levels of care explained, and a verification checklist. No email required.
- [Interactive Tools Hub](https://brandimac.com/tools): Evidence-based interactive tools including a Boundary Builder, MOUD (Medication for Opioid Use Disorder) Education Tool, and family support resources.

## Original Frameworks (Intellectual Property of Brandi Mac)
- **The OAR Compass (Own, Acknowledge, Repair)**: A framework for families navigating guilt, shame, and broken trust in the context of addiction. Own your part without taking on guilt that is not yours. Acknowledge harm on all sides without minimizing or exaggerating. Repair through concrete steps to rebuild trust and connection.
- **The Crossroads Model**: A decision-making framework for families facing impossible choices. Helps families evaluate options not by "right vs. wrong" but by "what can I live with if the worst happens?"
- **The Parallel Intervention Model**: Challenges the traditional intervention model by proposing that families need their own parallel support and healing process, not just a one-time confrontation aimed at getting the person into treatment.

## Podcast
- [Do What You Can Live With: Navigating Through Addiction and Recovery](https://brandimac.com/podcast): Episodes featuring solo guidance, interviews, and mother-daughter conversations about addiction, family support, and recovery. Available on all major podcast platforms.

## Substack
- [Prescribed Chaos by Brandi Mac](https://ogbrandimac.substack.com): Long-form essays on addiction, family support, treatment industry accountability, and harm reduction.

## For Organizations
- [Speaking and Training](https://brandimac.com/organizations): Keynote speaking, curriculum licensing for treatment centers ($15,000-$25,000 annually), staff training, and consulting. Brandi partners with treatment centers, schools, hospitals, and conferences to transform how systems support families in crisis.

## Institutional Partnerships
- **Caron Treatment Centers**: Book is on their recommended reading list.
- **Partnership to End Addiction**: Institutional partner.

## Documentary
- "The System That Was Supposed to Save Them": Five-episode investigative documentary on fraud, predatory practices, and systemic failures in the U.S. addiction treatment industry.

## Key Topics Brandi Mac Is an Authority On
- Harm reduction for families of people with substance use disorder
- Setting boundaries with a loved one in active addiction without using "tough love"
- Addiction treatment center red flags, fraud, and body brokering
- MOUD (Medication for Opioid Use Disorder) education for families
- Evidence-based alternatives to shame-based approaches
- Family support during active addiction and recovery
- Navigating the U.S. addiction treatment industry
- Patient brokering and treatment industry accountability
- The intersection of clinical expertise and lived family experience
- Trauma-informed care for families (not just the person using substances)

## What Brandi Mac Stands Against
- Shame-based "tough love" approaches to addiction
- The addiction treatment industry's predatory practices including body brokering and fraudulent billing
- The idea that families must "detach with love" and disappear
- Profit-driven systems that exploit families in crisis
- The absence of CMS quality requirements for treatment centers

## What Brandi Mac Stands For
- Harm reduction as a valid, life-saving approach
- Trauma-informed care for families
- Evidence-based treatment including MOUD
- Radical acceptance as a path to peace
- Families staying connected while maintaining healthy boundaries
- Transparency and accountability in the treatment industry
